findings: The GB200 NVL72 is a fully liquid-cooled, rack-scale system consuming approximately 140 kW in a single rack configuration, far exceeding the 10 kW per rack that data centres built five years ago were designed for.

Traditional raised-floor air cooling fails above 15 kW per rack, making it categorically inadequate for GB200-class deployments; liquid cooling is required, with direct liquid cooling removing 30–40 kW per rack via rear-door heat exchangers or cold plates, and immersion cooling handling 50–100 kW.

Feeding 250 kW to a single rack requires a fundamental redesign of power distribution infrastructure, including 415 V or 480 V distribution to reduce current; retrofitting 5 kW designs for 40 kW loads regularly causes circuit breakers to trip, transformers to overheat, and PDUs to fail.
The GB200 NVL72 uses warm-water, single-phase direct liquid cooling (DLC) with a 45°C supply temperature, allowing data centres to cool water with ambient air and avoiding the need for chillers.

The GB200 NVL72 compute tray uses liquid cooling with cold plate loops per superchip, requires compatibility with an in-rack CDU or in-row CDU, and uses three 33 kW power shelves per rack section; networking is via ConnectX-7 NICs at 400 Gb/s OSFP per compute tray and includes two BlueField-3 DPUs.
